text_original: Da You indicates that, (under the circumstances which it implies), there will be great progress and success.
text_normalized: Da You indicates that, (under the circumstances which it implies), there will be great progress and success.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=Judgment.

text_original: In the first NINE, undivided, there is no approach to what is injurious, and there is no error. Let there be a realisation of the difficulty (and danger of the position), and there will be no error (to the end).
text_normalized: In the first NINE, undivided, there is no approach to what is injurious, and there is no error. Let there be a realisation of the difficulty (and danger of the position), and there will be no error (to the end).
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=first.

text_original: In the second NINE, undivided, we have a large waggon with its load. In whatever direction advance is made, there will be no error.
text_normalized: In the second NINE, undivided, we have a large waggon with its load. In whatever direction advance is made, there will be no error.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=second.

text_original: The third NINE, undivided, shows us a feudal prince presenting his offerings to the Son of Heaven. A small man would be unequal (to such a duty).
text_normalized: The third NINE, undivided, shows us a feudal prince presenting his offerings to the Son of Heaven. A small man would be unequal (to such a duty).
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=third.

text_original: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject keeping his great resources under restraint. There will be no error.
text_normalized: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject keeping his great resources under restraint. There will be no error.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fourth.

text_original: The fifth SIX, divided, shows the sincerity of its subject reciprocated by that of all the others (represented in the hexagram). Let him display a proper majesty, and there will be good fortune.
text_normalized: The fifth SIX, divided, shows the sincerity of its subject reciprocated by that of all the others (represented in the hexagram). Let him display a proper majesty, and there will be good fortune.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fifth.

text_original: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows its subject with help accorded to him from Heaven. There will be good fortune, advantage in every respect.
text_normalized: The topmost NINE, undivided, shows its subject with help accorded to him from Heaven. There will be good fortune, advantage in every respect.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=top.
